questions.question: Name the three current Claude model tiers and one trade-off between them.
options: Opus (most capable, slower), Sonnet (balanced), Haiku (fastest, less capable)

questions.question: Which Claude.ai plans include access to Claude Cowork?
options: Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise (all paid plans)

questions.question: What is an Artifact? Name three Artifact types Claude can render.
options: A saved conversation — text, audio, video
A plugin output — charts, maps, tables
A rich inline output — code, documents/markdown, diagrams, HTML/React previews
A file attachment — PDF, DOCX, images

questions.question: What is the default file-size limit for a single attachment on Claude.ai?
List two reasons web search may not trigger automatically.
options: The web search toggle is disabled; the query doesn't signal need for current data
The user is on mobile; Claude is in dark mode
The file is too large; the model is Haiku
The Project has too many files; memory is disabled

questions.question: What is the maximum context size a Project can hold (in tokens)?
How does Project memory differ from global memory?
options: Project memory is faster than global memory
Project memory persists only within a specific Project; global memory persists across all conversations
Global memory is only for Enterprise plans

questions.question: Inside a Project, which takes precedence: Project custom instructions or user preferences?
options: It depends on the plan tier

questions.question: Does Incognito mode write to memory? Why or why not?
options: Yes, it writes to encrypted memory
No, Incognito mode disables all memory writing to ensure privacy
Yes, but only to Project memory
No, but it still reads from existing memory

questions.question: A user says their Project 'forgets' reference files mid-chat — what is the likely cause?
options: The files were deleted from the user's computer
Files may exceed the 200K token budget, or file ingest is incomplete
The user is on the Free plan
Memory is corrupted and needs reset

questions.question: Which surface does NOT support Claude Cowork?
options: All of the above support Cowork

questions.question: What should a support agent check first when a customer reports a 'login loop'?
options: Check if Claude is down globally
Clear cookies, try incognito mode, check IdP status
Ask the customer to reinstall the app

questions.question: Which file formats are supported for upload on Claude.ai?
options: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP), and audio

questions.question: What is the purpose of 'Research Mode' in Claude?
options: It enables Claude to access internal databases
It performs multi-step web searches and synthesizes findings with citations
It allows Claude to run experiments
It is the same as regular web search

questions.question: When should a user choose a single chat over a Project?
options: When they have recurring tasks with stable context
When working with a team on shared knowledge
For one-off questions, quick explorations, or tasks that don't need persistent context
When they need to upload more than 10 files

questions.question: What two YAML frontmatter fields are required in every SKILL.md?
How does Claude decide when to invoke a Skill?
options: The user must type a specific command
Claude reads the description field and matches it against user intent automatically
Skills run on a fixed schedule

questions.question: Why might a Skill with a too-generic description cause problems?
options: It will cause false positives — triggering on unrelated prompts
It will override all other Skills

questions.question: What is the precedence order for Skill levels?
Can a Skill include executable scripts? If so, what languages are supported?
options: Yes — Python, Node.js, and shell scripts

questions.question: What is the difference between a Connector and an MCP server?
options: Connectors are first-party (Anthropic-built, OAuth-based); MCP servers are open-protocol services built by anyone
MCP servers are first-party; Connectors are third-party
Connectors only work with Google products

questions.question: Name two Anthropic-built first-party Connectors.
Where does a user revoke a Connector's access?
options: Only through the external service's settings
In Claude's account settings or the integrations menu in chat

questions.question: A user gets '401 — token expired' — what should they do?
options: Trigger the re-authorization flow to refresh the token

questions.question: Can a third-party MCP App be auto-invoked without user consent?
options: No — MCP tools require per-tool user consent before execution
Yes, if the admin approves it

questions.question: What happens when two Skills have overlapping descriptions?
options: Both may trigger on the same prompt, causing confusion — descriptions should be made more distinct

questions.question: What scope options exist for Connectors?
What authentication method do Connectors use?
How can Enterprise admins control which Connectors are available?
options: They cannot — all Connectors are available to everyone
Through allow-lists, deny-lists, and audit logs

questions.question: What should you check first when a helper script in a Skill fails?
options: File permissions, shebang line, and installed dependencies

questions.question: What is the minimum required Node.js version for Claude Code?
Which npm package name installs the Claude Code CLI?
What does /init do in a new repo?
options: Creates CLAUDE.md and .claude/ directory for project configuration

questions.question: What slash command shows current session spend?
How would you reset authentication if it is stuck?
Where are sub-agent definitions stored?
What field in a sub-agent file controls when it is invoked?
What flag runs Claude Code non-interactively for CI?
Where are session logs written by default?
What does 'claude doctor' check?
options: Node version, auth status, network connectivity, and plugin health

questions.question: On Windows, where must Claude Code run?
What are Hooks in Claude Code?
options: Pre- and post-tool callbacks that execute automatically (e.g., linting after edits)

questions.question: How do you list installed plugins in Claude Code?
What causes 'command not found: claude' after installation?
options: Claude Code is not compatible with the OS
The npm global bin directory is not in the shell PATH

questions.question: What is the recommended solution for excessive context usage in Claude Code?
options: Use sub-agents to offload work and prune CLAUDE.md

questions.question: What are MCP's three core primitives?
Name two MCP transport options.
Which CLI command adds an MCP server to Claude Code?
What is the difference between an MCP 'tool' and a 'resource'?
options: A tool is an action the model can take; a resource is data the model can read
A resource is an action; a tool is data
Tools are for MCP; resources are for Connectors

questions.question: How does the user grant consent for an MCP tool to run?
options: Consent is automatic for all tools
Users approve each tool call via per-tool consent prompts
Admin pre-approves all tools at setup
Consent is handled by the MCP server

questions.question: On which plans is Cowork available?
options: Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise (paid plans only)

questions.question: What is the core difference between Chat and Cowork?
options: Chat is text-only; Cowork supports images
Chat is conversational; Cowork autonomously executes multi-step tasks in a sandboxed environment
Chat is free; Cowork is paid
Chat uses Haiku; Cowork uses Opus

questions.question: Name 4 of the 11 currently supported Cowork plugins.
Which OS-level virtualization does Cowork use on macOS?
What does Dispatch do?
options: It lets users initiate Cowork tasks from mobile

questions.question: What SDKs are available for authoring MCP servers?
options: Python SDK (mcp package) and TypeScript SDK
Only a REST API, no SDK

questions.question: What should you check first if an MCP server doesn't appear after adding it?
options: Check JSON config syntax and restart the Claude client

questions.question: What does the /schedule command do in Cowork?
options: Creates recurring automated tasks (e.g., daily report generation)

questions.question: Why might a Cowork VM fail to start?
options: The user's internet is too slow
Insufficient disk space, app needs restart, or macOS virtualization is not enabled
The user is on an iPad

questions.question: What output formats can Cowork produce?
options: Excel with formulas, PowerPoint, Word, PDFs, and images

questions.question: What is the top-level manifest file in a plugin?
Name 3 things a plugin can bundle.
options: Skills, MCP servers, sub-agents, and Connector configurations

questions.question: Where do private marketplaces come from?
options: Enterprise admins create internal catalogs of vetted plugins

questions.question: How do you list installed plugins in Claude Code?
What is the recommended way to avoid tool-name collisions between plugins?
options: Install only one plugin at a time
Use namespaced tool names in plugin.json

questions.question: Where are Claude Code session logs stored on macOS/Linux?
Which page hosts public incident reports for Anthropic services?
Name 4 items every escalation packet should contain.
options: User's full name, address, phone number, and SSN
Repro steps, timestamps, request IDs, and log excerpts
Screenshot, video recording, audio note, and drawing
Email thread, calendar invite, Slack link, and document

questions.question: Which log holds MCP server output by default?
options: Server stderr/stdout, captured by the host process

questions.question: What is the first triage step when a customer reports 'Claude is slow'?
options: Ask the customer to upgrade their plan
Tell the customer to clear their cache

questions.question: What security measures apply to public marketplace plugins?
options: Code signing, allow-lists, and review processes (automated + manual)
Users must review source code themselves

questions.question: What are the six common support issue categories?
options: Hardware, software, network, storage, display, audio
Authentication, network, rate limiting, model errors, plugin/skill issues, billing
Login, signup, payment, refund, cancel, upgrade
Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android, web

questions.question: Can plugins work across both Claude Code and Cowork?
options: Yes — the plugin.json manifest declares compatibility; some work in both
Only Enterprise plugins work across products
Only MCP-based plugins work across products

questions.question: What is the pass threshold for the final cumulative assessment?
A user's API key works in curl but not in their script. What should you check first?
options: Header name (x-api-key), anthropic-version header, model name string, and local proxy
Whether the API key has expired
